# Typography

The YaleSites type system is built around Yale's brand typefaces and a structured scale of sizes, weights, and styles. Use the **Headings: Typography** toolbar control to preview components in each heading font pairing.

## References

- [Typefaces](?path=/story/tokens-typography--type-faces) — The font families available in the design system and their intended roles.
- [Type Scale](?path=/story/tokens-typography--type-scale) — The full range of font sizes, from display headings down to small utility text.
- [Heading Styles](?path=/story/tokens-typography--heading-styles) — How the type scale applies to H1–H6, including weight, size, and line-height.
- [Body Styles](?path=/story/tokens-typography--body-styles) — Paragraph, lead text, caption, and other body-level type treatments.

## Heading Font Pairings

YaleSites supports multiple heading font pairings to accommodate different site personalities while staying within the Yale brand. The body font (Mallory) remains consistent across all pairings.

- **YaleNew (Old-Style Numerals)** — The default. Classic serif headings with traditional numeral styling.
- **YaleNew (Lining Numerals)** — Same typeface, modern numeral style for a cleaner look in data-heavy contexts.
- **Mallory** — Sans-serif headings for a more contemporary, minimal feel.
